description: Perform all medical, diagnostic, and surgical procedures considered essential for the area of practice.
fullText: Patient Care — Perform all medical, diagnostic, and surgical procedures considered essential for the area of practice.

description: Gather essential and accurate information about patients and their condition through history-taking, physical examination, and the use of laboratory data, imaging, and other tests.
fullText: Patient Care — Gather essential and accurate information about patients and their condition through history-taking, physical examination, and the use of laboratory data, imaging, and other tests.

description: Organize and prioritize responsibilities to provide care that is safe, effective, and efficient.
fullText: Patient Care — Organize and prioritize responsibilities to provide care that is safe, effective, and efficient.

description: Interpret laboratory data, imaging studies, and other tests required for the area of practice.
fullText: Patient Care — Interpret laboratory data, imaging studies, and other tests required for the area of practice.

description: Make informed decisions about diagnostic and therapeutic interventions based on patient information and preferences, up-to-date scientific evidence, and clinical judgment.
fullText: Patient Care — Make informed decisions about diagnostic and therapeutic interventions based on patient information and preferences, up-to-date scientific evidence, and clinical judgment.

description: Develop and carry out patient management plans.
fullText: Patient Care — Develop and carry out patient management plans.

description: Counsel and educate patients and their families to empower them to participate in their care and enable shared decision making.
fullText: Patient Care — Counsel and educate patients and their families to empower them to participate in their care and enable shared decision making.

description: Provide appropriate referral of patients including ensuring continuity of care throughout transitions between providers or settings, and following up on patient progress and outcomes.
fullText: Patient Care — Provide appropriate referral of patients including ensuring continuity of care throughout transitions between providers or settings, and following up on patient progress and outcomes.

description: Provide health care services to patients, families, and communities aimed at preventing health problems or maintaining health.
fullText: Patient Care — Provide health care services to patients, families, and communities aimed at preventing health problems or maintaining health.

description: Provide appropriate role modeling.
fullText: Patient Care — Provide appropriate role modeling.

description: Perform supervisory responsibilities commensurate with one's roles, abilities, and qualifications.
fullText: Patient Care — Perform supervisory responsibilities commensurate with one's roles, abilities, and qualifications.

description: Demonstrate an investigatory and analytic approach to clinical situations.
fullText: Knowledge for Practice — Demonstrate an investigatory and analytic approach to clinical situations.

description: Apply established and emerging biophysical scientific principles fundamental to health care for patients and populations.
fullText: Knowledge for Practice — Apply established and emerging biophysical scientific principles fundamental to health care for patients and populations.

description: Apply established and emerging principles of clinical sciences to diagnostic and therapeutic decision making, clinical problem solving, and other aspects of evidence-based health care.
fullText: Knowledge for Practice — Apply established and emerging principles of clinical sciences to diagnostic and therapeutic decision making, clinical problem solving, and other aspects of evidence-based health care.

description: Apply principles of epidemiological sciences to the identification of health problems, risk factors, treatment strategies, resources, and disease prevention/health promotion efforts for patients and populations.
fullText: Knowledge for Practice — Apply principles of epidemiological sciences to the identification of health problems, risk factors, treatment strategies, resources, and disease prevention/health promotion efforts for patients and populations.

description: Apply principles of social-behavioral sciences to provision of patient care, including assessment of the impact of psychosocial-cultural influences on health, disease, care-seeking, care-compliance, and barriers to and attitudes toward care.
fullText: Knowledge for Practice — Apply principles of social-behavioral sciences to provision of patient care, including assessment of the impact of psychosocial-cultural influences on health, disease, care-seeking, care-compliance, and barriers to and attitudes toward care.

description: Contribute to the creation, dissemination, application, and translation of new health care knowledge and practices.
fullText: Knowledge for Practice — Contribute to the creation, dissemination, application, and translation of new health care knowledge and practices.

description: Identify strengths, deficiencies, and limits in one's knowledge and expertise.

description: Set learning and improvement goals.

description: Identify and perform learning activities that address one's gaps in knowledge, skills, or attitudes.

description: Systematically analyze practice using quality-improvement methods, and implement changes with the goal of practice improvement.

description: Incorporate feedback into daily practice.

description: Locate, appraise, and assimilate evidence from scientific studies related to patients' health problems.

description: Use information technology to optimize learning.

description: Participate in the education of patients, families, students, trainees, peers, and other health professionals.

description: Obtain and utilize information about individual patients, populations of patients, or communities from which patients are drawn to improve care.

description: Continually identify, analyze, and implement new knowledge, guidelines, standards, technologies, products, or services that have been demonstrated to improve outcomes.

description: Communicate effectively with patients, families, and the public, as appropriate, across a broad range of socioeconomic and cultural backgrounds.

description: Maintain comprehensive, timely, and legible medical records.

description: Demonstrate compassion, integrity, and respect for others.

description: Demonstrate accountability to patients, society, and the profession.

description: Demonstrate sensitivity and responsiveness to a diverse patient population, including but not limited to diversity in gender, age, culture, race, religion, disabilities, and sexual orientation.

fullText: Professionalism — Demonstrate a commitment to ethical principles pertaining to provision or withholding of care, confidentiality, informed consent, and business practices, including compliance with relevant laws, policies, and regulations.
